title: Quiz: VQE for Portfolio Optimization
description: Test your understanding of VQE algorithm and its application to portfolio problems

questions.question: What does VQE stand for?
questions.explanation: VQE stands for Variational Quantum Eigensolver, an algorithm originally designed to find ground state energies of molecular Hamiltonians.

questions.question: What is the main difference between VQE and QAOA ansätze?

options.text: QAOA has problem-specific structure, VQE uses general parametrized circuits
VQE can only solve chemistry problems

questions.explanation: QAOA uses a structured ansatz based on the problem (alternating cost and mixer layers), while VQE uses flexible, hardware-efficient ansätze that can be adapted to any circuit design.

questions.question: How is a QUBO problem encoded for VQE?

options.text: As a sum of Pauli operators (Hamiltonian)

questions.explanation: For VQE, the QUBO is converted to a Hamiltonian expressed as a sum of Pauli operators (I, X, Y, Z and tensor products). VQE then finds the ground state of this Hamiltonian.

questions.question: What is a hardware-efficient ansatz?

options.text: An ansatz that uses fewer qubits
An ansatz designed to match the connectivity and native gates of specific quantum hardware
An ansatz that runs on classical computers

questions.explanation: Hardware-efficient ansätze are designed to work well on specific quantum devices by using gates native to that hardware and respecting qubit connectivity constraints.

questions.question: More parameters in a VQE ansatz always leads to better solutions.
questions.explanation: False. While more parameters can increase expressibility, they can also lead to barren plateaus (vanishing gradients), harder optimization, and increased susceptibility to noise.

questions.question: What is a barren plateau in VQE?

options.text: A region where the quantum computer runs slowly
A flat region in the parameter landscape where gradients vanish

questions.explanation: Barren plateaus are regions in the parameter space where the gradient of the cost function vanishes exponentially, making optimization extremely difficult.

questions.question: When might VQE be preferred over QAOA for portfolio optimization?

options.text: When hardware constraints require a specific circuit structure
When the problem has no constraints

questions.explanation: VQE's flexible ansatz design makes it preferable when you need to adapt to specific hardware connectivity or when you want to design constraint-preserving circuits.

questions.question: Which are valid ways to handle constraints in VQE? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Add penalty terms to the Hamiltonian

questions.explanation: Constraints can be handled through penalty terms (penalize violations), ansatz design (e.g., excitation-preserving circuits), or post-processing. Ignoring constraints would give invalid solutions.
